{"query":"Narendra Modi Ashadhi Bij Kutchi New Year greetings July 17 2026 message","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1815,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://ddnews.gov.in/en/pm-modi-extends-kutchi-new-year-greetings-on-ashadhi-bij/","title":"PM Modi extends Kutchi New Year greetings on Ashadhi Bij | DD News","content":"Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday extended greetings to the global Kutchi community on the occasion of Ashadhi Bij, which marks the Kutchi New Year.
Ram Ram, and many congratulations and warm wishes to everyone!”
The Prime Minister’s message highlighted the enduring cultural identity of the Kutchi community and acknowledged its widespread presence across India and around the world, where its members continue to preserve their traditions and heritage.
Ashadhi Bij is celebrated as the Kutchi New Year and holds special significance for the people of the Kutch region in Gujarat, as well as for the global Kutchi diaspora.

Copyright © 2026 DD News.","date":"2 days ago"},{"url":"https://www.mid-day.com/amp/news/india-news/article/pm-narendra-modi-wishes-kutchi-community-on-ashadhi-bij-with-heartfelt-message-23640050","title":"PM Modi extends Ashadhi Bij greetings to the global Kutchi community on Kutchi New Year","content":"2 days ago — PM Narendra Modi on Thursday extended warm greetings to the global Kutchi community on the occassion of Ashadhi Bij, which marks the Kutchi ...
Today, on Ashadhi Bij, the Kutchi New Year, Ram Ram and heartfelt congratulations to all Kutchi brothers and sisters living across the country and around the world," he wrote.
His greetings came after he had earlier extended wishes on the occasion of the Jagannath Rath Yatra.
The traditions associated with the Rath Yatra have inspired generations across India and the world.
The Prime Minister also prayed for the well-being of all and said: "May Mahaprabhu Jagannath bless everyone with good health, happiness and prosperity.
The festival coincides with the arrival of the monsoon season in the region and is considered an important occasion for farming communities.
It is celebrated with cultural programmes, community gatherings and the exchange of New Year greetings.
Meanwhile, the Jagannath Rath Yatra in Ahmedabad, regarded as the second-largest in the country after Puri in Odisha, also commenced on Ashadi Bij, attracting a large number of devotees.
